title: cli:cli/plugins-show
description: Defines the CLI contract for showing detailed plugin information. The command loads a plugin and displays its metadata, configSchema, and capabilities.
context: Defines the CLI contract for showing detailed plugin information. The command loads a plugin and displays its metadata, configSchema, and capabilities.

rules:
The command MUST accept a single plugin name as a positional argument:

```bash
specd plugins show <plugin>
```

The command MUST display:

- Plugin name
- Plugin type
- Version (from the npm package)
- Configuration schema (if any)
- Capabilities

The format MUST be machine-parseable when invoked with `--format json`.
If the plugin cannot be loaded, the command MUST emit an error message and exit with code 1.

constraints: The command MUST NOT modify any state.

scenarios.name: Show plugin details
then: plugin metadata, configSchema, and capabilities are displayed

scenarios.name: Plugin not found
when: specd plugins show nonexistent-plugin is invoked
